mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate reduces discount-rate pressure on equities and supports risk appetite, so lower policy rates predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher after monthly FEDFUNDS declines than after flat or rising FEDFUNDS, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%, tight policy suppresses liquidity and raises equity duration discounting, so the forward 20-day S&P 500 return is negative until the rate falls back below the threshold.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% do not produce negative average 20-day SPX returns, or if positive returns occur in more than 60% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate indicates weakening earnings and higher recession risk, so a 3-month increase in UNRATE predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not negative on average after UNRATE rises exceeding 0.3 percentage points over 3 months, or if the sign accuracy falls below 55% over any 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A falling PAYEMS series together with a rising UNRATE signals labor-market deterioration stronger than either series alone, which should compress earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-down and UNRATE-up condition does not outperform either proxy alone in predicting negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction coefficient is not materially different from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized rise in RSAFS and INDPRO indicates broadening real activity and revenue growth, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through stronger earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if joint increases in RSAFS and INDPRO do not correspond to above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term loses significance in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST below recent trend marks housing demand contraction and a broader cyclical slowdown, which should weigh on S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls > 10% below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST drawdowns greater than 10% versus a 6-month moving average are not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns, or if the conditional hit rate stays below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous pickup in INDPRO and GDPC1 signals stronger real output and capacity utilization, which should support equities over a 60-trading-day horizon by improving profit growth expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if joint strength in INDPRO and GDPC1 does not lead to positive average 60-day SPX returns, or if the combined signal underperforms a simple market mean over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a disinflation regime, a low CPIAUCSL together with an unchanged or falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers real-rate pressure and supports multiple expansion, so S&P 500 returns should be positive over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation trend falls below its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if disinflation episodes with stable or falling FEDFUNDS do not generate positive 5-day SPX returns, or if the interaction signal is negative in more than half of such episodes.
